sections.paragraph:
ἀναϰρίνω [ἀνά, κρίνω] ‘engage in careful inquiry’

—a. in respect to matters that cause some hesitation about taking a course of action: make a close study of, examine Ac 17:11; ask questions about, question 11:12 v.l.; 1 Cor 10:25, 27.

—b. in forensic context, of subjecting someone to judicial proceedings hold an inquiry, hear a case, put on trial Lk 23:14; Ac 4:9; 12:19; 24:8; 28:18. In extended forensic sense, examine, investigate 1 Cor 4:3f; 9:3; then of spiritual matters subject to spiritual inquiry 2:14f; 14:24.
